events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: PPE selection, respirator wrong cartridge color (ID). This means the respirator has an incorrect cartridge color for the contaminant/ID. Cartridge color coding is per ANSI/NIOSH standards. Wrong cartridge color indicates non-compliant PPE selection - using wrong cartridge could expose worker to wrong hazard. OSHA 1910 Subpart I requires proper PPE selection and respiratory protection (1910.134 actually, but Subpart I is general PPE). Respirator cartridge color coding matters for identifying correct protection.

Decision: fail. Risk: high (wrong respiratory protection could lead to exposure). Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: non-compliant, installed-defective. Percent complete: maybe 80. Confidence high.
